entries.definition:
كتاب الواو

وضم

(وعي) الواو والعين والياء: كلمةٌ تدلُّ على ضمِّ شيء. ووَعَيْتُ العِلْمَ أعِيهِ وَعْياً. وأوْعَيْتُ المتاعَ في الوِعاء أُوعيه. قال:

* والشَّرُّ أخبَثُ ما أوعَيْتَ من زادِ *

وأمَّا الوَعَى فالجَلَبَةُ والأصوات. وهو عندنا من باب الإبدال، والأصل الغين. والوعية: الصَّارخَة، من الوَعَى. ويقولون: لا وَعْيَ عَنْ كذا.

a warning to those people who were there at the time and to those
who came after them, and a lesson to all who are mindful of God
2 an admonition, exhortation (16:125) ادْعُ إِلَى سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِالْحِكْمَةِ
وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ call to the way of your Lord with wisdom and kindly
exhortation.
entries.definition_english:
a warning to those people who were there at the time and to those
who came after them, and a lesson to all who are mindful of God
2 an admonition, exhortation (16:125) ادْعُ إِلَى سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِالْحِكْمَةِ
وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ call to the way of your Lord with wisdom and kindly
exhortation.

entries.definition:
w–ʿ–y vessel, container; awareness, comprehension; noise; to
collect, to gather, to comprise; to comprehend, to be aware, to be
alert; (of a wound) to secrete; to exhaust. Of this root, five forms
occur seven times in the Qur’an: تَعِي taʿī once; أَوْعَى ʾawʿā twice;
وَاعِيَة wāʿiyatun once; وِعَاء wiʿāʾ twice and أَوْعِيَة ʾawʿiyatun once.

تَعِي taʿī [imperf. of v. وَعَى waʿā, trans.] to grasp, to perceive, to
retain, to take in (69:12) وَتَعِيَهَا أُذُنٌ وَاعِيَةٌ and attentive ears might
take it in.

أَوْعَى ʾawʿā [v. IV, trans.] 1 to keep from others, to hoard,
(70:18) وَجَمَعَ فَأَوْعَى amasses [wealth] and hoards it 2 to hide in
one’s heart, to harbour (84:23) وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يُوعُونَ God knows best
what they keep hidden inside [their hearts].

وَاعِيَة wāʿiyatun [fem. act. part.] perceptive, attentive, grasping
(69:12) and attentive ears might take it in.

وِعَاء wiʿāʾ [n. pl. أَوْعِيَة ʾawʿiyatun] baggage, pack, travel gear
(12:76) فَبَدَأَ بِأَوْعِيَتِهِمْ قَبْلَ وِعَاءِ أَخِيهِ so he began with their packs before
the pack of his brother.
